diagrams.title: System Call Flow Between User and Kernel Modes
diagrams.description: Shows the transition from user mode to kernel mode when a system call is invoked
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how user programs request kernel services and the mode switching mechanism

diagrams.title: Categories of System Calls
diagrams.description: Six categories of system calls with examples for each category
elements: Process Control: fork, exec, exit, wait
File Management: open, read, write, close
diagrams.purpose: Organize and memorize all types of system calls by category

diagrams.title: Process Creation using fork() and exec()
diagrams.description: Shows how fork() creates a child process and exec() loads a new program
diagrams.purpose: Understand the sequence of system calls for creating and running new programs
